summary:
Endoscopy is preferred due to its accuracy in diagnosing Zenker’s diverticulum and allowing biopsy when needed; other modalities like CT/MRI are less precise but may be used as complementary tools.
Endoscopy provides definitive diagnosis while offering potential concurrent treatment benefits not available through alternative investigations such as MRI/CT scans which serve more supportive roles rather than primary diagnostics here given their lower specificity levels regarding anatomical detail required clinically speaking during differential considerations involving esophageal pathology
